notes: Fog-of-war regression suite. The DEFAULT pass captures the static grid-reveal scene (fog_zoom2/4/8_origin): a visible reveal circle, an explored memory band, and unexplored black, plus the #2008 column-cull pillar canary — the general FOG_TO_TRIXEL + per-voxel-column-clip guard. The 'edge' EXTRA RUN re-runs with --edge-zoom (a static analytic vision circle at the origin with VOXEL objects straddling its boundary) and gates the fog CUT-FACE cross-section (#2125, epic #2124 P1): a boundary-cut voxel object caps with a filled interior wall instead of a see-through hole. The scene also carries a thick ground slab spanning the disc; its cut RIM gates the screen-space cross-section silhouette (#2124 follow-up): the own-column drop keeps the boundary cells the disc crosses (nearest-cell test) so FOG_TO_TRIXEL trims the rim to the analytic disc at game resolution instead of stair-stepping on the voxel lattice. The 'detached' EXTRA RUN re-runs with --detached-edge (the same origin vision circle but the boundary-straddling object is a world-placed DETACHED_REVOXELIZE solid on its own canvas + pool) and gates the detached cross-section (#2127, epic #2124 P3): a detached canvas carries no fog of its own, so this proves the world fog + observers thread into its STAGE_1/2 dispatch and each voxel's world column is recovered from worldCellOffset — it cross-sections like the GRID twin instead of rendering whole. The 'edge_smooth' EXTRA RUN re-runs with --edge-smooth — the SAME geometry as --edge-zoom but a wide soft vision-circle band (Mode B, #2126 P2): the object silhouette + cut wall fade on the smooth disc curve like the floor (the own-column drop keeps partially-revealed columns so FOG_TO_TRIXEL trims them), rather than ending hard at the binary radius. The 'edge_sdf_blocker' EXTRA RUN re-runs with --edge-sdf-blocker — the SAME --edge-zoom voxel scene PLUS one SDF box carrying C_LightBlocker{blocksLOS_=true} straddling the -Y disc arc (#2247): #2247 reported an SDF blocker's fog-hidden half black-banding the cut instead of capping. Master's geometric cut cap (#2274) is content-independent — the box's rendered surface caps at the disc boundary regardless of any occlusion bitfield, so its -Y half fills with the toned cut wall like the -X voxel slab. This is the ENABLED-path gate proving a blocksLOS_ SDF straddling the disc still caps (the cap does not special-case blockers) — a scene WITHOUT the box (the other runs) proves nothing about it. The 'edge_zcost' EXTRA RUN re-runs with --edge-zcost — the vision disc as an XY radius with a Z-COST HEIGHT penalty (#2260): the observer sits at floor level, a central tall voxel pillar at the observer's XY has its base revealed and its top penalized past the disc (dist_xy + zCost*|z - observerZ|) so it fades then drops, and a low cube at the same XY band stays fully revealed. This is the ENABLED-path gate for the height penalty (every other run passes zCost 0, so they prove nothing about it); those other runs are the byte-identity gate that zCost 0 is a no-op. Each extra run emits exactly its kEdgeShots/kDetachedEdgeShots/kEdgeSmoothShots/kEdgeSdfBlockerShots/kEdgeZCostShots, so capture_offset 0 maps them 1:1. All gated scenes are static + cardinal-yaw-0 with identity-rotation detached solids (the source re-voxelize path — a spinning solid round-to-cell speckles, #1557), verified bit-deterministic run-to-run on macOS (0 drift). The demo's other modes (--moving-observer orbit, --player-walk) are intentionally NOT gated here — they advance a per-frame float center, so they are non-deterministic by construction (manual / attach-screenshots inspection aids). Reference PNGs live in sibling subdirs per CMake preset (macos-debug/, linux-debug/) since Metal and OpenGL produce pixel-different output; linux-debug/ is captured on a Linux/OpenGL host (fleet:needs-linux-smoke) — a backend without committed references SKIPS rather than fails, so bless it there with render-verify --update-references. Shot order mirrors kShots[] / kEdgeShots[] / kDetachedEdgeShots[] / kEdgeSmoothShots[] / kEdgeSdfBlockerShots[] / kEdgeZCostShots[] in main.cpp.
